title: Area and Perimeter Complete Question Bank
subject: Mathematics

questions.question: The area of the floor of a rectangular room is 630 m². If the breadth of the floor is 18 m, find its length.
A square is formed by bending a 112 cm long wire. The area of this square is:
A rectangular path of 60 m length and 3 m width is covered by square tiles of side length 25 cm each. Find the number of tiles used to construct the path.
A boy runs around a square field of area 625 m². How much distance will he run if he takes four rounds of the field?
A room is 8 m long and 5 m broad. Find the cost of carpeting its floor at the rate of ₹150 per m².
The length and breadth of a rectangle are 18 m and 8 m respectively. If the area of a square is four times the area of this rectangle, find the length of each side of the square.
The area of a rectangle is 450 m² and its breadth is 18 m. The perimeter of the rectangle is:
How much distance Aman will cover if he takes three rounds of a square park whose length of each side is 50 m?
The perimeter of a square park is 60 m. Find the cost of levelling its floor at the rate of ₹12 per square metre.
A rectangular park has a perimeter of 80 m and a length of 26 m. What is its area?
A square playground has each side of length 20 metres. If the cost of fencing around the playground is ₹12 per metre, find the total cost of fencing.
A square tile has each side of length 10 cm, and an equilateral triangular plate has each side of length 13 cm. Which has a greater perimeter and by how much?
If the length of each side of a pentagon is 18 m, find its perimeter.
An equilateral triangle has each side of length x cm. If its perimeter is 60 cm, what is the value of x?
The perimeter of a square is 96 cm. If the length of each side is reduced by 6 cm, what is the new perimeter of the square?
A rectangle has a perimeter of 56 cm. If the length is 2 cm more than the breadth, find the dimensions of the rectangle.
The area of a shape is the amount of ______ it covers.
The formula for the area of a rectangle is: Area = ______ × ______.
A square whose each side is 6 cm has an area of ______ cm².
If a rectangle has an area of 36 cm² and its width is 4 cm, then its length is ______ cm.
The formula for the area of a square is: Area = ______ × ______.
If l is the length and b is the breadth of a rectangle, then its perimeter is ______.
If each side of an equilateral triangle is ‘a’, then its perimeter is ______.
The perimeter of a square with each side of length x is ______.
If each side of a hexagon is A, then its perimeter is ______.
If each side of a pentagon is M, then its perimeter is ______.
The perimeter of a rectangle is the sum of all its ______.
A square has ______ equal sides.
The area of a square of side 8 cm is ______ cm².
The perimeter of a square of side 9 cm is ______ cm.
The area of a rectangle with length 12 cm and breadth 5 cm is ______ cm².
The perimeter of a rectangle with length 10 cm and breadth 6 cm is ______ cm.
A triangle with all equal sides is called an ______ triangle.
The area of a figure is measured in ______ units.
Perimeter is measured in ______ units.
The perimeter of a hexagon with side 7 cm is ______ cm.
